11.title: Baby Swallows & Hiccups
11.subtitle: Practicing for life on the outside.
11.keyDevDesc: Baby starts swallowing fluid and hiccuping.
11.partnerSymptomsDesc: Morning sickness may start to ease up.
babyDevBullets: The fetus is learning to swallow amniotic fluid.
Vocal cords are forming.
Hair follicles are developing on the head and body.
emotionalScripts: You've been so strong through these first rough weeks.
practicalTasks: Double check that the 12-week NT scan is booked.
Start planning the nursery layout conceptually.
relationshipIdeas: Go for a gentle walk together and talk about how you're both feeling.

bondingTasks.title: Heartbeat app
bondingTasks.text: Listen for the heartbeat if you have an at-home doppler (but don't panic if you can't find it).

optionalBuy: Belly butter for stretch mark prevention
medicalRoadmap: Nuchal Translucency (NT) scan usually occurs between weeks 11 and 13.
questions: Do we need a full anatomy scan later if the NT scan is normal?
takeaways: The hardest physical weeks for her are almost over.
The baby is practicing swallowing.

12.title: End of First Trimester
12.subtitle: The risk of miscarriage drops significantly.
12.dadPriorityDesc: You made it through the toughest part.
12.partnerSymptomsDesc: The placenta takes over hormone production.
babyDevBullets: Reflexes are developing fast: fingers can open and close.
Intestines are moving into the baby's abdomen.
Kidneys are excreting urine into the bladder.

symptomTable.name: Heartburn
symptomTable.help: Keep Tums handy, avoid spicy dinners

emotionalScripts: We did it. We survived the first trimester.
practicalTasks: Finalize your announcement plan (if sharing at 12 weeks).
Research daycare or nanny options—waitlists can be a year long!
relationshipIdeas: Celebrate reaching the 12-week milestone with a nice dinner.
medicalRoadmap: NT scan to measure fluid at the back of baby's neck.
questions: When do we get the NIPT/NT results?
takeaways: Miscarriage risk drops below 2%.
Placenta takes over, giving her more energy.

13.title: The Threshold
13.subtitle: Welcome to the honeymoon phase.
13.dadPriorityDesc: Gear up for a more active second trimester.
13.partnerSymptomsDesc: Uterus shifts up and out of the pelvis.
babyDevBullets: Unique fingerprints are forming on tiny fingertips.
Baby can make facial expressions like smiling and frowning.
Vocal cords are fully developed (though there's no air to make sound).

symptomTable.name: Round Ligament Pain
symptomTable.help: Suggest moving slowly when standing up

emotionalScripts: Your bump is starting to show, you look amazing.
practicalTasks: Take a baseline 'bump photo'.
Start looking into childbirth classes.
relationshipIdeas: Plan a 'babymoon' (a final vacation before the baby arrives).

bondingTasks.title: Babymoon Planning
bondingTasks.text: Pick a destination that is Zika-free and relaxing.

medicalRoadmap: Typically a month break until the next 16-week appointment.
takeaways: The first trimester is officially over.
Unique fingerprints are formed.

14.title: Energy Returns
14.subtitle: Second trimester officially begins.
14.keyDevDesc: Neck elongates, baby holds head up.
14.dadPriorityDesc: Capitalize on her returning energy to get things done.
14.partnerSymptomsDesc: Nausea gone, energy back, glowing skin.
babyDevBullets: The neck is getting longer, allowing the baby to hold its head straighter.
Spleen starts producing red blood cells.
Baby can squint, frown, and grimace.

symptomTable.name: Increased Appetite
symptomTable.why: Nausea is gone, baby needs calories
symptomTable.help: Keep the fridge stocked with healthy snacks

emotionalScripts: It is so good to see you feeling like yourself again.
practicalTasks: Begin clearing out the room that will become the nursery.
Finalize the budget for big-ticket baby items (stroller, crib).
relationshipIdeas: Go out on a real date now that she can tolerate restaurant smells.
medicalRoadmap: Second trimester blood tests (e.g., AFP for neural tube defects).
takeaways: The golden trimester is here.
Get big projects started now.

15.title: Sensing Light
15.subtitle: Even with closed eyes, baby senses the outside world.
15.keyDevDesc: Baby reacts to bright lights outside the belly.
15.dadPriorityDesc: Help her adjust to her changing center of gravity.
15.partnerSymptomsDesc: Increased blood volume swells mucous membranes.
babyDevBullets: Baby's eyes are sensitive to light even though eyelids are fused shut.
Legs are growing longer than arms.
Baby is covered in lanugo (fine downy hair) to keep warm.

symptomTable.name: Pregnancy Rhinitis
symptomTable.help: Buy a humidifier for the bedroom

emotionalScripts: Let's take a break and elevate your feet.
practicalTasks: Set up a humidifier in the bedroom to help with congestion.
Start interviewing pediatricians.
relationshipIdeas: Read a parenting book chapter out loud together before bed.

bondingTasks.title: Flashlight Game
bondingTasks.text: Shine a flashlight on her belly to see if baby reacts (though it might still be too early to feel).

questions: Can we hear the heartbeat at the next visit?
takeaways: Baby can sense light.
Nasal congestion is a very common pregnancy symptom.

16.title: Hearing Dad's Voice
16.subtitle: Baby is listening.
16.keyDevDesc: Bones in the ear harden, baby can hear.
16.partnerSymptomsDesc: She might feel the first tiny flutters of movement.
babyDevBullets: Tiny bones in the ear harden; baby can hear your voice and mom's heartbeat.
The heart is pumping about 25 quarts of blood a day.
Scalp patterning has begun, though hair isn't visible yet.
emotionalScripts: I love talking to our baby. It makes it feel so real.
practicalTasks: Talk, sing, or read to the belly daily.
Look into life insurance and setting up a will.
relationshipIdeas: Listen to a favorite album together and tell the baby about it.

bondingTasks.title: Voice Recognition
bondingTasks.text: Lower your voice and talk directly to the bump so baby learns your pitch.

medicalRoadmap: 16-week routine checkup (weight, blood pressure, Doppler heartbeat).
questions: Are we tracking on weight gain appropriately?
takeaways: The baby can hear you now.
She may feel the first kicks ('quickening').

17.title: Practice Swallowing
17.subtitle: Getting ready for the real world.
17.dadPriorityDesc: Order the crib and plan the room.
17.partnerSymptomsDesc: Skin stretches rapidly over the growing uterus.
babyDevBullets: Baby's skeleton is transforming from soft cartilage to hard bone.
Baby is practicing swallowing and sucking reflexes.
Fat stores are beginning to develop under the skin.

symptomTable.name: Itchy Skin
symptomTable.help: Apply thick cocoa butter or belly oil

emotionalScripts: Your body is doing exactly what it needs to do.
practicalTasks: Order the crib and mattress (delivery can take weeks).
Make sure you both have a clear plan for maternity/paternity leave.
relationshipIdeas: Rub belly oil on her stomach for her before bed.

bondingTasks.title: Crib Assembly
bondingTasks.text: Clear the space for the crib and gear.

mustBuy: Belly oil or lotion for stretch marks
medicalRoadmap: Start preparing for the Anatomy Scan at week 18-20.
takeaways: Cartilage is turning into bone.
Skin care for her stretching belly is essential.

18.title: The Anatomy Scan Window
18.subtitle: The biggest ultrasound of the pregnancy.
18.keyDevDesc: Nerves are coated in myelin to speed messages.
18.dadPriorityDesc: Be there for the anatomy scan.
18.partnerSymptomsDesc: Cardiovascular system adjusting to blood volume.
babyDevBullets: Nervous system is rapidly maturing.
If it's a girl, her uterus and fallopian tubes are formed.
If it's a boy, his genitals may be visible on an ultrasound.

symptomTable.name: Dizziness upon standing
symptomTable.help: Remind her to stand up slowly, carry snacks

emotionalScripts: I am so excited to see every little detail of our baby.
practicalTasks: Attend the Anatomy Scan (Level 2 Ultrasound). Do not miss this.
If finding out the sex, decide if you want the tech to tell you or write it in an envelope.
relationshipIdeas: Go out for a celebratory meal after the anatomy scan.

bondingTasks.title: Gender Reveal
bondingTasks.text: Plan how you want to learn the gender (privately or with family).

medicalRoadmap: Anatomy Scan: Tech measures all organs, brain, heart, and limbs to ensure proper development.
questions: Is the placenta in a safe position?
takeaways: This is the most detailed ultrasound you will have.
You can find out the sex of the baby.

19.title: Baby Kicks Soon
19.subtitle: Sensory development kicks into high gear.
19.keyDevDesc: A waxy coating protects baby's skin in the fluid.
19.dadPriorityDesc: Start feeling her belly for movement.
babyDevBullets: Brain is designating specialized areas for smell, taste, hearing, vision, and touch.
Baby is covered in vernix caseosa, a greasy white substance protecting skin from amniotic fluid.
Kidneys continue to produce urine.

symptomTable.name: Leg Cramps
symptomTable.why: Weight gain, nerve compression, mineral shortage

emotionalScripts: Let me know the second you feel a kick so I can try to feel it too.
practicalTasks: Research and register for infant CPR classes.
Start building the baby registry.
relationshipIdeas: Spend 10 minutes each night with your hand on her belly waiting for a kick.

bondingTasks.title: Calf Massage
bondingTasks.text: Learn how to rub out a leg cramp effectively.

medicalRoadmap: Review anatomy scan results with the OB.
questions: Should we be taking magnesium for the leg cramps?
takeaways: Sensory brain development is peaking.
You might feel the first kick from the outside soon.

20.title: Halfway There!
20.subtitle: You are at the exact midpoint of pregnancy.
20.sizeDesc: ~16.4 cm (crown to rump), ~25.6 cm (head to heel)
20.keyDevDesc: Baby's first poop is forming in the intestines.
20.partnerSymptomsDesc: The expanding uterus pushes the navel outward.
babyDevBullets: Measurement changes from 'crown-to-rump' to 'head-to-heel'.
Baby has working taste buds and drinks amniotic fluid (tasting what mom eats).
Meconium (dark, sticky first poop) is forming in the bowels.

symptomTable.name: Shortness of Breath
symptomTable.help: Don't rush her; carry the heavy items

emotionalScripts: We are officially halfway to meeting our baby.
practicalTasks: Publish the baby registry.
Plan the baby shower (or assist the friends/family planning it).

bondingTasks.title: Halfway Celebration
bondingTasks.text: Take a milestone photo to commemorate 20 weeks.

medicalRoadmap: Fundal height (measuring the belly) becomes a standard part of OB checks from now on.
questions: Is fundal height measuring on track?
takeaways: You are halfway to birth.
Baby is practicing digestion and tasting mom's meals.
